Think it was maybe a year ago. I'm sure very few of you have seen me do this I went through this this on chart that I refer to as the cycle of beliefs How beliefs get formed and how they end up tripping you up So let me just back up and explain you know what a belief really is a Belief is essentially it's something that you think is true. It's something it's something you think to be true. I Think I'm great at I believe I'm great at tennis. I believe in the world. I think it's true It's not a fact beliefs are not facts of life Beliefs are the things about the world about ourselves that we think to be true. You have something called Identity beliefs I'm too old. I'm too young. I'm too fat. I'm too thin. I'm handsome. I'm ugly I'm gregarious and outgoing. I'm a loner. I'm a I'm a introvert beliefs There's all beliefs. There's nothing empirically right or wrong. They're belief systems now on The flip side the question is well what causes beliefs to be formed Well at the end of the day What really forms beliefs are these root? experiences that we have That serve as is almost like it starts with a root experience and then once something happens to us that experience Changes the way we perceive things and we start actually cutting out things that don't agree with this belief And we narrow and narrow our field of focus and we only see things and notice things that line up with our beliefs And we delete things that don't That's how it works now. I'm gonna get back to that in a second Let me just give you the other type of beliefs of the other type one was identity the second is capability beliefs What you're capable of I am capable of being getting rich I'm capable of making my own way in life. I'm capable of closing anyone who's closable I'm capable of massive compassion I'm capable of being a great public speaker if you don't think you'd be a great public speaker I got new so you guess what you know you ain't gonna want to do public speaking if you don't think you can close You're not gonna try to close but you don't you don't try things that we think we're not good at We don't try we don't go we don't go for the brass ring if we don't believe we're gonna get that brass ring You know why it makes sense because again, it's almost positive intent the brain says What's the point? Why do all the work? Why should I go and spend a hundred two or a thousand man hours and what I really know my hot-hearts I don't really have what it takes. I'm not really that sort of guy or girl I don't really possess all the requisite skills. That's just I don't see myself succeeding The capability belief right now. Let me show you this chart of beliefs here and how it works So I'll try my hand at being in this world class honestly again. He's by the way There's a reason I'm super terrible honest. So watch here's the way it works you have four boxes and Essentially this first box is what we have is called pure potential Meaning it's like we're born on day one and what perfect now. I understand some people are Got to get belted very bad that and their physical, you know Limitations really and there are those people this does not refer to that right so if you God God Hope you're not that person but if you have a mentally challenged in some way You know or you're physically challenged and you want to become basketball player You get it's this reality here, too But I'm told there's a general way we move through the world right on day one. We have generally pure potential Right, that's day one. They were born then comes day two of our lives right after our parents Don't take it away because here's what happens you start with pure potential and then as you start moving through life You take actions, okay? Okay, and based on those actions right here you take actions, right and Based on those actions you are going to get what you're going to get results Both positive and negative and then based on those results You're gonna form a belief about who you are and what you're capable of for example Let's say you go out and you're in third grade and the teacher says to you, okay I want you to come Jimmy time for you to give your speech in front of the class now and Jimmy stands up. He's nervous because no one really taught Jimmy had a few public speaking Jimmy didn't prepare himself. He hasn't he didn't really write out his speech He doesn't have this cocharing thoughts and assertions to make Jimmy's about to go up and wing it And he's not one of those people who was I was like that who can wing it and do great Jimmy is not a natural born closer or speaker. So what happens Jimmy's average? So Jimmy walks up in front of the room his heart's beating a mile a minute. He's the way for slow but as he starts talking the kids like Everyone's making faces and Jimmy starts to feel pain and uncomfortability Jimmy got a negative result Get a very poor result As Jimmy watches the people snicker and laugh at him He feels pain inside emotional hurt right and he forms a belief saying what? Well number one people are mean but to I'm just not good at public speaking I'm not I'm not I'm not a good speaker. I'm not good. I hate and he goes and then he goes I hate public speaking. I never want to do this again and Jimmy starts to develop a fear of public speaking and Actual limitation because what happens is because of this belief and the fear associated What happens as he goes back to do it again? His potential was diminished because next time Fresh with the memory of the disaster of last time what happens? Well people typically do not very rare that some will actually practice something They suck at and unless they're really being pushed by someone in which case it's a very good thing But what happens typically is the next time Jimmy's I'm not good at that. I don't want to do that again He won't prepare. He won't study which I overcome it. All right, and next time he comes up Paralyzed even more so what happens to his was as takes the action again his results even worse What happens his belief is reinforced? I am really bad. I suck. I'm the worst Let me give you a bad example here with me. I'm the terrible artist, right? I really am right? Let me ask you an honest question. I mean, you know, walk the heart do you think that I'm missing an art gene Is there something wrong with me? I'm a pretty talented person normally, right? Am I missing the gene that I just simply can't draw a line. I can't draw a bowl of fruit I can't paint that is something wrong with me. Am I deficient and suppose my DNA up? No It's not at all. Let me tell you what happened to me. I'll tell you exactly what happened when I was in kindergarten I had this teacher named mrs. Roskin. All right, and mrs. Roskin She was a nice little plumpy mrs. Roskin, right? And she had a bowl of fruit and it was time for people to do painting and crayons, right? And I was sitting at my desk with a girl sitting here a girl sitting here these two little bastards natural artists, right? She's like, okay, everybody here's your crayons. Everyone draw the bowl of fruit. So I'm like, okay, whatever. I feel pretty good I start drawing him. I'm not a natural artist. Was it a natural God-given gift to me, right? So I start drawing and after I own one like looks like someone throw up my Bombard on the paper, right and I glance to my right. I'm like, oh I'm like Sherry She drew a picture. It looks like holy I start getting a little nervous, right? I'm like, oh, oh, so wrong. You're right. I look at the girl. Oh my god It's even better. Mrs. Roskin said oh Sherry. Oh my god. What a budding little Picasso you are She told my god good girl. She right then she looks to the oh my god Janine Oh my god, she goes to me. Well, Jordan, I Guess you're just not gonna be I guess this is just not for you and in that moment my heart sank I remember this day. I still remember it. I'm like, oh my god I'm not now just you're very good. You're a very good speaker because I because I already believe that even in kindergarten I already was so verbal over the top verbal and advanced at that point And I was still talking down my teachers in kindergarten So I was a gift I had since I was born, right? Just you're just not meant to be an artist just stick with stick with math and science Jordan You're speaking because I was very smart with that stuff But this no, but you don't think I could learn can draw of course like a remember the guy on TV I don't be still around you say he said okay now This is your world draw your painting your palette and he tray and draw great and you can learn you had a strategy for art This strategy is for everything. You know what I think you understand the self reinforcing nature of beliefs now the good news is is that Once you understand this and accept it as being true and because it's just a belief right?
